questions.text: Which of the following is a prokaryote?
questions.explanation: Bacteria are prokaryotes (no well-defined nucleus). Amoeba, Yeast, and Spirogyra are eukaryotes.

questions.text: The five kingdom classification was proposed by:
questions.explanation: R.H. Whittaker proposed the five kingdom classification in 1969: Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia.
Fungi are heterotrophic as they lack chlorophyll and obtain nutrition from dead and decaying organic matter.

questions.text: The cell wall of fungi is made up of ________.
questions.explanation: The fungal cell wall is made of chitin, a complex polysaccharide. Plant cell walls are made of cellulose.
